Package org.eventb.core.pm

Interface IProofState

All Superinterfaces:
IProofTreeChangedListener
All Known Implementing Classes:
ProofState
public interface IProofState extends IProofTreeChangedListener
Common protocol for proof states that correspond to proofs of an obligation. This contains the proof tree, the set of cached hypotheses, the set of searched hypotheses.
Since:
1.0

    Methods inherited from interface org.eventb.core.seqprover.IProofTreeChangedListener

    proofTreeChanged

  • Method Details

    • loadProofTree

      void loadProofTree(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Load the corresponding proof tree from the disk. The current proof tree will be discarded. The current post-tactic will be applied at the root of the new proof tree. The current node will be set to the first pending sub-goal or the root if there is no pending subgoals. Finally, both the cached and searched hypotheses are set to empty.

      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problems occurred during the loading of the proof tree.

    • isClosed

      boolean isClosed() throws RodinDBException
      Check if the current proof is closed (there is no pending subgoal left). The current proof is closed if:
      • There is a proof tree and the proof tree is closed.
      • If there is no proof trees, the status on disk for the corresponding obligation is closed.

      Returns:
      true if the current proof is closed. Return false otherwise.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - is some problems occurred.

    • isPending

      boolean isPending() throws RodinDBException
      Tells whether the current proof is pending.
      The current proof is pending if:
      • there is a proof tree and the proof tree confidence is lower or equals to than IConfidence.REVIEWED_MAX
      • there is no proof tree, the status on disk of the sequent corresponding to the proof obligation obligation is not discharged.
      Returns:
      true if the current proof is pending, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      Since:
      3.2

    • getPSStatus

      IPSStatus getPSStatus()
      Get the corresponding proof state status IPSStatus. This must not be null.

      Returns:
      a IPSStatus

    • getFormulaFactory

      FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()
      Returns the formula factory of the current proof tree.
      Returns:
      the formula factory of the proof tree if any, null otherwise
      Since:
      3.0

    • getProofTree

      IProofTree getProofTree()
      Get the current proof tree.

      Returns:
      the current proof tree if any. Return null otherwise.

    • getCurrentNode

      IProofTreeNode getCurrentNode()
      Get the current proof tree node.

      Returns:
      the current proof tree node if any. Return null in the case where there is no proof tree or no current proof tree node.

    • setCurrentNode

      void setCurrentNode(IProofTreeNode newNode) throws RodinDBException
      Set the current proof tree node. Do nothing if the new node is not contained in the current proof tree.

      Parameters:
      newNode - a proof tree node.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problems occurred.

    • getNextPendingSubgoal

      IProofTreeNode getNextPendingSubgoal(IProofTreeNode node)
      Get the next pending subgoal from the input node. This method does not change the current proof tree node.

      Parameters:
      node - a proof tree node
      Returns:
      a next pending proof tree node if any. Return null otherwise.

    • getSelected

      Iterable<Predicate> getSelected()
      Return an iterable on the selected hypotheses at the current proof tree node. If there is no proof tree, returns an iterable on an empty collection.
      Returns:
      an iterable on a collection of selected hypotheses at the current proof tree node
      Since:
      2.4

    • filterHypotheses

      Collection<Predicate> filterHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> preds)
      Filters out predicates that are not actual hypotheses of the current proof tree node. If there is no proof tree, returns an empty collection.
      Parameters:
      preds - a collection of predicates to filter
      Returns:
      a sub-collection of the given collection containing only hypotheses at the current proof tree node
      Since:
      2.4

    • addAllToCached

      void addAllToCached(Collection<Predicate> hyps)
      Add a collection of hypotheses to the cache.

      Parameters:
      hyps - a collection of hypotheses.

    • removeAllFromCached

      void removeAllFromCached(Collection<Predicate> hyps)
      Remove a collection of hypotheses from the cache.

      Parameters:
      hyps - a collection of hypotheses

    • getCached

      Collection<Predicate> getCached()
      Return the collection of cached hypotheses.

      Returns:
      a collection of cached hypotheses.

    • removeAllFromSearched

      void removeAllFromSearched(Collection<Predicate> hyps)
      Remove a collection of hypotheses from the set of searched hypotheses.

      Parameters:
      hyps - a collection of hypotheses

    • getSearched

      Collection<Predicate> getSearched()
      Return the collection of searched hypotheses.

      Returns:
      a collection of searched hypotheses.

    • setSearched

      void setSearched(Collection<Predicate> searched)
      Set the set of searched hypotheses.

      Parameters:
      searched - the set of new searched hypotheses.

    • isDirty

      boolean isDirty()
      Check if the proof has some unsaved changes, i.e. the proof tree has been modified compared with the one on disk.

      Returns:
      true if the proof has some unsaved changes. Return false otherwise.

    • setProofTree

      void setProofTree(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Set the current proof tree to the disk. The dirty flag is set to false.

      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problem occurred during the saving.

    • setDirty

      void setDirty(boolean dirty)
      Set the dirty flag of the proof.

      Parameters:
      dirty - true to indicate that there are some unsaved changes. false otherwise.

    • proofReuse

      void proofReuse(IProofMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Reuse the current proof tree with the obligation. The proof tree must exist.

      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problems occurred

    • proofRebuilt

      void proofRebuilt(IProofMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Rebuilt the current proof tree with the obligation. The proof tree must exist.

      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problems occurred.

    • isUninitialised

      boolean isUninitialised()
      Check if the proof state is uninitialised, i.e. the proof tree has not been loaded.

      Returns:
      true if uninitialised. Return false otherwise.

    • isSequentDischarged

      boolean isSequentDischarged() throws RodinDBException
      Check if the status of sequent on disk is discharged.

      Returns:
      true if the status sequent on disk is discharged. Return false otherwise.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problems occurred

    • isProofReusable

      boolean isProofReusable() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Check if the current proof is reusable with the proof obligation.

      Returns:
      >true if the proof is reusable. Return false otherwise.
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if some problems occurred accessing the database, or if the current proof is invalid

    • unloadProofTree

      void unloadProofTree()
      Unload the current proof tree, i.e. the proof state will become uninitialised.

    • back

      void back(IProofTreeNode node, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Backtrack from the given node. This is equivalent to pruning at the parent of the input node.

      Parameters:
      node - a proof tree node.
      monitor - a proof monitor.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problems occurred.

    • setComment

      void setComment(String text, IProofTreeNode node) throws RodinDBException
      Setting the comment at a proof tree node.

      Parameters:
      text - the comment string.
      node - a proof tree node.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problems occurred.
